text: Here we describe an iron-catalyzed method for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkenes and alkynes. This approach enables the formation of a broad range of perfluoroalkylated alkenyl and alkynyl iodides, including those bearing trifluoromethyl (CF3) groups, in high yields with excellent functional group tolerance. These crucial building blocks can be further transformed via cross-coupling reactions to produce complex perfluoroalkylated molecules in a highly efficient manner.

These crucial building blocks can be further transformed via cross-coupling reactions to produce complex perfluoroalkylated molecules in a highly efficient manner.
